title: gaya gracilipes var. genuina
intro: Gaya gracilipes var. genuina, also known as the 'Mountain Gaya', is a rare and lesser-known plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the Mountain Gaya plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although its uses and effects should be further studied and confirmed.
